title: Physical AI Conference Comes to San Jose as Robotics & Autonomous AI Go Mainstream
optimized_headline: Physical AI Conference Comes to San Jose as Robotics & Autonomous AI Go Mainstream
raw_excerpt: The Physical AI Conference shaping the future of robotics, autonomous systems and real-world AI deployment lands in Silicon Valley this May, bringing together the engineers, builders and AI pioneers turning intelligence into physical action.  Physical AI Expo North America will take place on May 18–19, 2026 at the San Jose McEnery Convention Center, uniting global AI innovators, robotics leaders,
raw_body:
The Physical AI Conference shaping the future of robotics, autonomous systems and real-world AI deployment lands in Silicon Valley this May, bringing together the engineers, builders and AI pioneers turning intelligence into physical action.  Physical AI Expo North America will take place on May 18–19, 2026 at the San Jose McEnery Convention Center, uniting global AI innovators, robotics leaders, […]
The post Physical AI Conference Comes to San Jose as Robotics & Autonomous AI Go Mainstream  appeared first on AI News.
speedrun: The Physical AI Conference is set for May 18–19, 2026, in San Jose, California, focusing on robotics and autonomous systems. This event will gather engineers and innovators who are advancing AI's role in physical applications. With the increasing integration of AI in everyday technology, this conference highlights a pivotal moment in making AI more accessible and practical. It matters now as industries look to harness these technologies for real-world solutions.
why_it_matters: Engineers and developers will gain insights and networking opportunities, potentially accelerating project timelines and innovation in robotics.
The event signals a broader shift towards integrating AI into physical systems, reflecting increasing investment and interest in autonomous technologies across various sectors.

lenses.eli12: The Physical AI Conference is like a big meeting where people who build robots and smart machines gather to share ideas. They will discuss how to make AI work in real life, which is becoming more common. This matters because it shows that AI is not just for computers anymore; it's moving into our homes and workplaces.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, this conference presents a chance to learn about the latest trends in robotics and AI. Understanding how these technologies can be applied may help in developing cost-effective solutions that meet user needs. Attending could inspire new product ideas or partnerships that enhance market competitiveness.
lenses.engineer: The conference will showcase advancements in robotics and autonomous systems, likely featuring discussions on cutting-edge models and deployment strategies. Engineers can expect to learn about new benchmarks in efficiency and performance. This event could highlight the practical applications of AI technologies, pushing forward the boundaries of what is possible in physical AI.
